name: Old English Sherry Cheese Puffs
recipeIngredient: 5 oz. Jar Old English (sharp, pasteurized process cheese spread)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease a cookie sheet.
In a medium saucepan, heat water, salt, and margarine until boiling.
Add flour and sherry, stirring constantly until the mixture forms a ball and leaves the sides of the pan.
Remove from heat; stir in 1/4 cup cheese spread.
Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. The mixture should be smooth and glossy.
Drop dough by teaspoonfuls onto prepared cookie sheet.
Bake for 15-20 minutes until puffed and golden brown.
Remove from the cookie sheets. Let cool slightly; make a small slit in the side of each puff to release steam and prevent sogginess.
Let cool completely.
In a small bowl, combine remaining cheese spread, chopped stuffed green olives, and sherry; blend until smooth and fluffy.
Slit puffs carefully; remove any soft filaments of dough.
Fill with about one teaspoonful of the cheese mixture.
Makes 18 appetizer puffs.
This is a wonderful holiday appetizer featuring rich cheese puff pastry.
